title: List Directory Contents (Linux)
description: List information about the files in the current directory. Taught in Linux Fundamentals Part 1.
when_to_use: To view files and directories.

flag_descriptions.-la: List all files including hidden, in long format

title: Change Directory (Linux)
description: Change the current working directory.
when_to_use: To navigate the filesystem.

title: Concatenate Files (Linux)
description: Read and output the contents of a file to the terminal.
when_to_use: To quickly view the contents of a small text file.

title: Find Files (Linux)
description: Search for files in a directory hierarchy.
when_to_use: To locate a specific file, such as a flag.txt in a CTF.

title: Search Text (Linux)
description: Print lines matching a pattern.
when_to_use: To search for specific keywords, like 'password', in configuration files.

optional_flags.description: Invert match (show lines that do NOT match)

title: Change Permissions (Linux)
description: Modify the read/write/execute permissions of a file.
when_to_use: To make an uploaded script executable.

title: Change Owner (Linux)
description: Change the file owner and group.
when_to_use: To take ownership of a file.

title: Switch User (Linux)
description: Run a command with substitute user and group ID.
when_to_use: To switch to another user account during lateral movement.

title: Execute as Root (Linux)
description: Execute a command as the superuser.
when_to_use: To run a command with elevated privileges.

optional_flags.description: List commands the user can run via sudo (Great for PrivEsc!)

title: Download File (wget)
description: Non-interactive network downloader.
when_to_use: To download exploits or enumeration scripts to a target machine.

title: Extract Archive (tar)
description: Extract a compressed tarball archive.
when_to_use: To decompress a .tar.gz file.

title: SSH Login
description: Log into a remote machine using SSH.
when_to_use: To gain a secure shell on a target after discovering credentials.

title: List Directory (Windows)
description: Displays a list of files and subdirectories in a directory.
when_to_use: To view files in CMD.

title: Read File (Windows)
description: Displays the contents of a text file.
when_to_use: To read a flag.txt file on a Windows target.

title: System Information (Windows)
description: Displays detailed OS configuration information.
when_to_use: To gather OS version and hotfix data for privilege escalation.

title: Local Users (Windows CMD)
description: Display user accounts on the computer.
when_to_use: To enumerate valid user accounts.

title: Local Groups (Windows CMD)
description: Display local groups on the computer.
when_to_use: To see which users have admin privileges.

title: List Running Processes (Windows)
description: Displays a list of currently running processes.
when_to_use: To identify anti-virus, security software, or vulnerable services running.

title: Local Users (PowerShell)
description: Enumerate local users using PowerShell.
when_to_use: Modern way to enumerate users on Windows.

title: Check IP Address (Linux)
description: Show routing and network devices.
when_to_use: To find your TryHackMe VPN IP.

title: Check IP Address (Windows)
description: Displays all current TCP/IP network configuration values.
when_to_use: To check IP addressing and DNS settings.

title: ICMP Ping
description: Send ICMP ECHO_REQUEST to check if a host is alive.
when_to_use: To verify network connectivity.

title: Network Statistics (netstat)
description: Displays active TCP connections, listening ports, and routing tables.
when_to_use: To identify what services are listening internally on a compromised host.

flag_descriptions.-a: Displays all active connections and listening ports
flag_descriptions.-n: Displays active TCP connections numerically (no DNS resolution)
flag_descriptions.-o: Displays active TCP connections and includes the process ID (PID) for each connection (Windows)

title: Nmap: Top 1000 Ports
description: Basic port scan for the 1,000 most common ports.
when_to_use: The first step in enumerating a target.

optional_flags.description: Attempt to determine the version of the services running
Skip host discovery (treat the host as online)

title: Nmap: All 65535 Ports
description: Scan every TCP port to ensure nothing is missed.
when_to_use: When the basic scan finishes and you want to look for obscure ports.

title: Nmap: UDP Scan
description: Scan for open UDP ports (requires root/sudo).
when_to_use: To check for services like SNMP, DNS, or TFTP running on UDP.

optional_flags.description: Only scan the top 100 most common UDP ports (much faster)

title: Nmap: Aggressive Scan
description: Combines OS detection, version scanning, script scanning, and traceroute.
when_to_use: To gather as much information as possible quickly.

flag_descriptions.-A: Enable OS detection, version detection, script scanning, and traceroute

title: Check HTTP Headers (cURL)
description: Fetch only the HTTP response headers.
when_to_use: To quickly check the Server version or missing security headers.

title: Web Directory Brute-Forcing (Gobuster)
description: Use Gobuster to find hidden directories and files on a web server.
when_to_use: To discover hidden admin panels or interesting files.

optional_flags.description: Also search for specific file extensions

title: Fuzzing Directories (ffuf)
description: Fast web fuzzer written in Go. Used similarly to Gobuster.
when_to_use: A faster alternative to Gobuster for discovering web paths.

flag_descriptions.-u: Target URL (must contain the keyword FUZZ)

title: Basic SQL Injection (sqlmap)
description: Automated tool to detect and exploit SQL injection flaws.
when_to_use: When you suspect a URL parameter (e.g., ?id=1) is vulnerable to SQL injection.

optional_flags.description: Never ask for user input, use the default behavior

title: Web Server Scanner (Nikto)
description: Scans web servers for outdated software and dangerous files.
when_to_use: For a quick vulnerability scan of a web server.

title: Check MD5 Hash (Linux)
description: Compute and check MD5 message digest.
when_to_use: To verify the integrity of a file or identify an MD5 hash.

title: Base64 Encode/Decode
description: Encode or decode data and print to standard output.
when_to_use: To quickly decode a base64 string found during enumeration.

title: Crack Passwords (John the Ripper)
description: Crack password hashes using a wordlist.
when_to_use: To crack captured password hashes (like Linux /etc/shadow dumps).

title: Crack Passwords (Hashcat)
description: Advanced password recovery utility.
when_to_use: For offline password cracking when you need GPU performance.

title: Brute-Force SSH (Hydra)
description: Use Hydra to guess the password for an SSH service.
when_to_use: When you know a username and want to brute-force their SSH password.

title: Start Metasploit
description: Launch the Metasploit Framework console.
when_to_use: To search for and execute pre-built exploits (like EternalBlue).

optional_flags.description: Quiet mode (don't print the startup banner)

title: Netcat Listener
description: Start a Netcat listener to catch reverse shells.
when_to_use: When executing a reverse shell payload on a target, you need this listening on your machine to catch the connection.

flag_descriptions.-lvnp: Listen, Verbose, No DNS resolution, Port number

title: Capture Packets (tcpdump)
description: A command-line packet analyzer.
when_to_use: To monitor live network traffic for suspicious activity.

flag_descriptions.-i: Specify the network interface to listen on

optional_flags.description: Show the packet payload in both HEX and ASCII

title: Analyze PCAP (TShark)
description: Terminal-based Wireshark. Analyze saved packet captures.
when_to_use: To analyze network traffic captures without a GUI.

flag_descriptions.-r: Read from a saved PCAP file

optional_flags.description: Apply a display filter (e.g., only show HTTP traffic)

title: Run Snort IDS
description: Run the Snort Intrusion Detection System on a PCAP file.
when_to_use: To analyze traffic against IDS rules to detect malicious behavior.
